query: US National Interagency Fire Center NIFC current National Preparedness Level July 2026, is it PL4 or PL5, current large fire acreage and resource commitment status
answer:
## Summary

**The NIFC National Preparedness Level in July 2026 is PL4 — not PL5.**

This is confirmed directly by the official NICC **Incident Management Situation Report dated Sunday, July 12, 2026 (0730 MDT)**, whose header reads "National Preparedness Level 4." No geographic area is at PL5; two areas (Great Basin and Rocky Mountain) are individually at PL4.

**Current large fire status (July 12, 2026):**
- **44 uncontained large fires** (83 total active large incidents)
- 12 new large incidents, 7 contained in the prior day
- **696,739 cumulative acres** on active large incidents

**Resource commitment:**
- **15,150 total personnel** (+741 from prior day)
- **316 crews, 952 engines, 143 helicopters**
- 15 Complex Incident Management Teams, 1 NIMO
- 2 MAFFS C-130 military airtankers activated (CA Air National Guard)

**Season context:** 2026 is running above average — 38,956 fires YTD (130% of 10-yr avg) and 3.58 million acres (137% of 10-yr avg). The Great Basin and Rocky Mountain areas are driving demand, with the largest active fires being Babylon (UT, 104,783 ac), Aspen Acres (CO, 97,505 ac), and Cottonwood (UT, 97,278 ac).

All data comes from the authoritative primary source (the NIFC/NICC IMSR PDF), cross-referenced against the NIFC National Fire News page.
